Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for emails (via search_emails with query "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat" or "compliance") to find the PHMSA extension letter from Linda Castellano, submission requirements, Greg Pallone's confirmation email about the Tanaka placarding certificate (from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com), and DOT-related correspondence.
OE 2: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages with search_query "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat" or "compliance") to find Elena's company-wide compliance policy post in #general, Chloe's updates on documentation progress, and Blessing's admission that she only received a verbal placarding acknowledgment from the Swift driver for the Tanaka move and never obtained the signed certificate.
OE 3: Search Linear for DOT compliance issues (via linear_list_issues with query related to "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat") to find the four flagged move tickets: OPS-1 (Zhao/GreenStack), OPS-2 (Tanaka/Mosaic), OPS-3 (Patel/NorthWind), OPS-4 (Reyes/GreenStack) under the DOT Compliance Audit project.
OE 4: Read Linear comments (via linear_list_comments) on the DOT compliance tickets, critically on OPS-3 (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, Raj Patel) to discover Chloe's comment that the signed chain-of-custody log cannot be located despite the ticket being marked Done; on OPS-1 (issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, Mei-Lin Zhao) to find Chloe's comment (comment_chloe_swift_confirmation) discussing Greg Pallone's confirmation from Swift and cross-referencing Zhao and Patel shipments. Note: OPS-2 (Tanaka) has no comments in the data. Tanaka documentation status is determined from the ticket description and the related ticket OPS-5 (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert, found via linear_list_issues with the DOT Compliance Audit project filter, state: Todo).
OE 5: Search or list Airtable relocation records (via airtable_list_bases -> airtable_list_tables -> airtable_list_records with table_name "Relocations") to find coordinator assignments: Javier Morales for Tanaka, Blessing Okafor for Patel, Fatimah Al-Rashidi for Zhao, and Javier Morales for Reyes.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for the DOT penalty accrual (via quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_accounts with criteria related to "DOT" or "accrual" or "penalty") to find bill ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 ($50,000, coded to the "Regulatory Compliance Expense" account) and the related "Regulatory Compliance: DOT Penalties (Pending)" liability account (acct_dot_penalty_accrual, balance $50,000), then assess whether the accrual amount is appropriate given current documentation gaps.
OE 7: Look up contact details (via contacts_search_contacts with query "Javier Morales" and "Blessing Okafor") to confirm their email addresses (javier.morales@moveops.com, blessing.okafor@moveops.com) before sending coordinator emails. Also, look up Rachel Whitfield (via contacts_search_contacts with query "Rachel Whitfield") to confirm rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com.
OE 8: Send an email (via send_email, sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: [javier.morales@moveops.com]) about the missing signed hazmat placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic Robotics Columbus-to-Seattle move, with end-of-week urgency.
OE 9: Send an email (via send_email, sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: [blessing.okafor@moveops.com]) about the missing signed chain-of-custody log for the Raj Patel/NorthWind Technologies Chicago-to-Denver move, with end-of-week urgency.
OE 10: Search for emails (via search_emails with query "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal") to find the counter-offer thread (Rachel's 12% counter on April 19, Mina's final 10% + Preferred Partner offer on April 19), and confirm no email from rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com after April 19.
OE 11: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages with search_query "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal") to check for any signal on the board decision.
OE 12: Search CRM for the Vectral deal (via crm_search_deals with dealname related to "Vectral") to find deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (stage: at_risk) and review engagement history. Also search CRM engagements (via crm_list_engagements) for the Vectral company to find the "Awaiting Board Decision" engagement and confirm no update since April 19.
OE 13: Search Linear for expense platform/ExpenseBot tickets (via linear_list_issues with query related to "expense" or "Vectral config" or "auto-categorizer") to find ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config, Vectral config fix) and related tickets. Read comments (via linear_list_comments) on ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) to find Lena's deploy verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified) confirming both ENG-214 and ENG-215 config fixes were deployed and verified in production, stating "Closing ENG-214" but neither ticket status was actually updated to Done in Linear.
OE 14: Log a status update on the Vectral CRM deal via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body describing current status: no Rachel response, ExpenseBot fix deployed but tickets not closed, auto-decisions disabled, recommendation for direct follow-up) and, if warranted, update the deal stage via crm_update_deal_stage.
OE 15: Send an email (via send_email, sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: [rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com], subject: related to "Vectral Renewal" or "Checking In" or similar), checking in on the board decision around April 23, mentioning the expense platform fixes have been addressed, and referencing the Preferred Partner terms.
OE 16: Search for emails (via search_emails with query "Terraform" or "Mina Hashimoto" or "Catalina" or "Rachel Nguyen") to find the reply-all incident thread, Catalina's apology, Mina's introduction email (email-terraform-mina-intro-001), and Rachel Nguyen's response (email-terraform-rachel-details-001) with relocation details for 3 employees (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi).
OE 17: Search CRM (via crm_search_deals with dealname "Terraform" to find deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1, then use the company_id "company-terraform-digital" to look up the company via crm_get_company. Note: the CRM company name is "Vantage Distributed", not "Terraform Digital", so crm_search_companies with name "Vantage Distributed" is the correct company search) for Terraform Digital deals and engagements to find the incident resolution engagement (coordinator hire authorization, tiered pricing decision) and the Q2 relocation batch deal (deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1, stage: qualification). Also, use crm_list_engagements with company_ids ["company-terraform-digital"] to review engagement history.
OE 18: Search Airtable (via airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Client Accounts", field_name "Client", value "Terraform Digital") for the Terraform Digital client record (recTerraformDigital01) to confirm Onboarding status with Mina as AM.
OE 19: Search Linear (via linear_list_issues with query "Terraform" or "coordinator") and read comments (via linear_list_comments) on relevant Terraform-related issues to discover that the dedicated relocation coordinator role authorized by Elena has not been posted. David Chen's comment on April 24 (linear_comment_155d3aca8415) flags the "unposted Terraform coordinator role as unavailable capacity," and Chloe Vance's comment on April 24 escalates the capacity risk and asks leadership what happened to the hire.
OE 20: Create a Linear ticket (via linear_create_issue, team: "team_operations", priority: 2 (High)) for the unresolved Terraform coordinator hire, title referencing "Terraform Digital" and the coordinator role.
OE 21: Post a status update (via conversations_add_message) to #general (channel C001) covering all three threads: DOT compliance documentation status with gaps identified, Vectral renewal with Rachel's non-response, and Terraform Digital handoff status with the coordinator hire gap.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for emails (via search_emails with query "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat" or "compliance") to find the PHMSA extension letter from Linda Castellano, submission requirements, Greg Pallone's confirmation email about the Tanaka placarding certificate (from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com), and DOT-related correspondence.
OE 2: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages with search_query "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat" or "compliance") to find Elena's company-wide compliance policy post in #general, Chloe's updates on documentation progress, and Blessing's admission that she only received a verbal placarding acknowledgment from the Swift driver for the Tanaka move and never obtained the signed certificate.
OE 3: Search Linear for DOT compliance issues (via linear_list_issues with query related to "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat") to find the four flagged move tickets: OPS-1 (Zhao/GreenStack), OPS-2 (Tanaka/Mosaic), OPS-3 (Patel/NorthWind), OPS-4 (Reyes/GreenStack) under the DOT Compliance Audit project.
OE 4: Read Linear comments (via linear_list_comments) on the DOT compliance tickets, critically on OPS-3 (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, Raj Patel) to discover Chloe's comment that the signed chain-of-custody log cannot be located despite the ticket being marked Done; on OPS-1 (issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, Mei-Lin Zhao) to find Chloe's comment (comment_chloe_swift_confirmation) discussing Greg Pallone's confirmation from Swift and cross-referencing Zhao and Patel shipments. Note: OPS-2 (Tanaka) has no comments in the data. Tanaka documentation status is determined from the ticket description and the related ticket OPS-5 (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert, found via linear_list_issues with the DOT Compliance Audit project filter, state: Todo).
OE 5: Search or list Airtable relocation records (via airtable_list_bases -> airtable_list_tables -> airtable_list_records with table_name "Relocations") to find coordinator assignments: Javier Morales for Tanaka, Blessing Okafor for Patel, Fatimah Al-Rashidi for Zhao, and Javier Morales for Reyes.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for the DOT penalty accrual (via quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_accounts with criteria related to "DOT" or "accrual" or "penalty") to find bill ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 ($50,000, coded to the "Regulatory Compliance Expense" account) and the related "Regulatory Compliance: DOT Penalties (Pending)" liability account (acct_dot_penalty_accrual, balance $50,000), then assess whether the accrual amount is appropriate given current documentation gaps.
OE 7: Look up contact details (via contacts_search_contacts with query "Javier Morales" and "Blessing Okafor") to confirm their email addresses (javier.morales@moveops.com, blessing.okafor@moveops.com) before sending coordinator emails. Also, look up Rachel Whitfield (via contacts_search_contacts with query "Rachel Whitfield") to confirm rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com.
OE 8: Send an email (via send_email, sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: [javier.morales@moveops.com]) about the missing signed hazmat placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic Robotics Columbus-to-Seattle move, with end-of-week urgency.
OE 9: Send an email (via send_email, sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: [blessing.okafor@moveops.com]) about the missing signed chain-of-custody log for the Raj Patel/NorthWind Technologies Chicago-to-Denver move, with end-of-week urgency.
OE 10: Search for emails (via search_emails with query "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal") to find the counter-offer thread (Rachel's 12% counter on April 19, Mina's final 10% + Preferred Partner offer on April 19), and confirm no email from rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com after April 19.
OE 11: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages with search_query "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal") to check for any signal on the board decision.
OE 12: Search CRM for the Vectral deal (via crm_search_deals with dealname related to "Vectral") to find deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (stage: at_risk) and review engagement history. Also search CRM engagements (via crm_list_engagements) for the Vectral company to find the "Awaiting Board Decision" engagement and confirm no update since April 19.
OE 13: Search Linear for expense platform/ExpenseBot tickets (via linear_list_issues with query related to "expense" or "Vectral config" or "auto-categorizer") to find ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config, Vectral config fix) and related tickets. Read comments (via linear_list_comments) on ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) to find Lena's deploy verification comment (comment_lena_deploy_verified) confirming both ENG-214 and ENG-215 config fixes were deployed and verified in production, stating "Closing ENG-214" but neither ticket status was actually updated to Done in Linear.
OE 14: Log a status update on the Vectral CRM deal via crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body describing current status: no Rachel response, ExpenseBot fix deployed but tickets not closed, auto-decisions disabled, recommendation for direct follow-up) and, if warranted, update the deal stage via crm_update_deal_stage.
OE 15: Send an email (via send_email, sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: [rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com], subject: related to "Vectral Renewal" or "Checking In" or similar), checking in on the board decision around April 23, mentioning the expense platform fixes have been addressed, and referencing the Preferred Partner terms.
OE 16: Search for emails (via search_emails with query "Terraform" or "Mina Hashimoto" or "Catalina" or "Rachel Nguyen") to find the reply-all incident thread, Catalina's apology, Mina's introduction email (email-terraform-mina-intro-001), and Rachel Nguyen's response (email-terraform-rachel-details-001) with relocation details for 3 employees (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi).
OE 17: Search CRM (via crm_search_deals with dealname "Terraform" to find deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1, then use the company_id "company-terraform-digital" to look up the company via crm_get_company. Note: the CRM company name is "Vantage Distributed", not "Terraform Digital", so crm_search_companies with name "Vantage Distributed" is the correct company search) for Terraform Digital deals and engagements to find the incident resolution engagement (coordinator hire authorization, tiered pricing decision) and the Q2 relocation batch deal (deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1, stage: qualification). Also, use crm_list_engagements with company_ids ["company-terraform-digital"] to review engagement history.
OE 18: Search Airtable (via airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Client Accounts", field_name "Client", value "Terraform Digital") for the Terraform Digital client record (recTerraformDigital01) to confirm Onboarding status with Mina as AM.
OE 19: Search Linear (via linear_list_issues with query "Terraform" or "coordinator") and read comments (via linear_list_comments) on relevant Terraform-related issues to discover that the dedicated relocation coordinator role authorized by Elena has not been posted. David Chen's comment on April 24 (linear_comment_155d3aca8415) flags the "unposted Terraform coordinator role as unavailable capacity," and Chloe Vance's comment on April 24 escalates the capacity risk and asks leadership what happened to the hire.
OE 20: Create a Linear ticket (via linear_create_issue, team: "team_operations", priority: 2 (High)) for the unresolved Terraform coordinator hire, title referencing "Terraform Digital" and the coordinator role.
OE 21: Post a status update (via conversations_add_message) to #general (channel C001) covering all three threads: DOT compliance documentation status with gaps identified, Vectral renewal with Rachel's non-response, and Terraform Digital handoff status with the coordinator hire gap.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via send_email) from "elena.rostova@moveops.com" to "javier.morales@moveops.com".
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✗✗ 50%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['javier.morales@moveops.com'], which succeeded with email_id '306b5bad2d054a7e8ef69f4fe2dc6667'.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a send_email tool call with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['javier.morales@moveops.com'] about the missing placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics move.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['javier.morales@moveops.com'], which succeeded (email_id returned).
✗Run #4The agent never sent an email to javier.morales@moveops.com. Instead, it sent the coordinator email to chloe.vance@moveops.com with blessing.okafor@moveops.com CC'd. The Airtable record (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) clearly shows Javier Morales as the assigned coordinator for the Keiko Tanaka move, but the agent directed the email to Chloe (manager) rather than to Javier (the move's actual coordinator).
✗Run #5No email was sent to javier.morales@moveops.com. The agent emailed blessing.okafor@moveops.com instead, because it read the Linear ticket description which mentioned Blessing Okafor, but failed to check the Airtable Relocations record (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) which shows Javier Morales as the assigned coordinator for the Tanaka move.
✗Run #6The agent never sent an email to javier.morales@moveops.com. It sent only one coordinator email (to blessing.okafor@moveops.com) about the Tanaka placarding cert gap. The agent apparently did not consult Airtable's Relocations table thoroughly enough to identify Javier Morales as the coordinator for the Tanaka/Mosaic Robotics move.
2 An email was sent (via send_email) from "elena.rostova@moveops.com" to "blessing.okafor@moveops.com".
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['blessing.okafor@moveops.com'], which succeeded with email_id 'e1703e3bfdeb4ac5871badcae6d189f2'.
✗Run #2No email was sent to blessing.okafor@moveops.com. The agent marked the Raj Patel / NorthWind move as complete and never identified the chain-of-custody log gap that Chloe noted in Linear comments (which the agent did not read). Therefore, no email was triggered for that move.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['blessing.okafor@moveops.com'], which succeeded.
✗Run #4No direct email was sent to blessing.okafor@moveops.com about the Raj Patel/NorthWind move. Blessing was only CC'd on the email about the Keiko Tanaka placarding cert. The agent never discovered the Patel chain-of-custody log gap (because it didn't call linear_list_comments) and therefore never sent an email to Blessing about that issue.
✓Run #5An email was sent via send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com (confirmed in the trajectory). While the email was about the wrong issue (Tanaka placarding cert rather than Patel chain-of-custody log), the criterion only asks if the email was sent to this address.
✓Run #6The agent did send an email via send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com. The email was sent (though it was about the Tanaka placarding cert rather than the Patel chain-of-custody log, the criteria only checks whether the email was sent to Blessing from Elena).
3 An email was sent (via send_email) from "elena.rostova@moveops.com" to "rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com".
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com'], which succeeded with email_id '6173eb27cabd4abea266512f2eb7e3cc'.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a send_email tool call with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com'] as a follow-up on the board decision and Vectral renewal.

✓Run #2The final response directly assesses Marcus's $50K accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) as 'appropriately conservative' and recommends 'no changes needed', confirming it is adequate given current circumstances.
✗Run #3The final response states the $50K accrual 'is likely understated' and asks Marcus to reassess upward, rather than identifying it as adequate given one confirmed gap (Tanaka) and one disputed issue (Patel). The agent over-escalated based on misidentifying Zhao as also having gaps.
✓Run #4The final response states: 'Marcus's $50,000 accrual (booked by Hana) is appropriate... No change recommended.' This clearly assesses the accrual as adequate given current status.
✓Run #5The final response states 'Marcus's $50,000 accrual is reasonable as a single-violation placeholder' and explains the conditions under which it could be reversed or might be insufficient, giving a clear assessment that it is appropriate given current status.
✓Run #6The final response states 'The $50K accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) is correctly sized. It reflects one confirmed gap at $50K/violation... No change needed right now.'
